definition: Planned Industrial District. A zoning category that allows industrial uses, including data centers under Ord 2024-07.
Limited Manufacturing District. The zoning category Ord 2023-09 amended to allow data centers as a permitted use.
Community Reinvestment Area. An Ohio program that allows local governments to grant property tax abatements to encourage development.
Tax Increment Financing. An Ohio mechanism that diverts new property tax revenue from a development to pay for infrastructure tied to it.
Million gallons per day. A standard unit for municipal water and wastewater capacity.
Megawatt. A unit of electrical power. A large data center can use tens to hundreds of megawatts continuously.
Non-disclosure agreement. The village signed one with Amazon Data Services in July 2023; the mayor stated the village sent non-renewal notice.
Committee of the Whole. A council session where every council member acts as a committee member, used in Commercial Point for in-depth review of items before formal council votes.
The detailed engineering and design drawing of a specific project, reviewed by the village separately from the underlying zoning.
A temporary pause on accepting or approving certain applications. The current proposal at council would pause new data centers.
